underlying: VoteHub time-adjusted average for Donald Trump's approval rating for Apr 30, 2026 (checked May 1, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET)
current_value_source: VoteHub's reported recent net approval moved from -15.3 to -11.8 (so approve ~43%); RCP 40.7, Silver Bulletin 39, FiftyPlusOne 37 — VoteHub historically runs slightly above SB/FPO and slightly below RCP, around 41-43.
method: Resolves YES if VoteHub's Apr-30 time-adjusted approve % is > 38.7. Threshold 38.7 was likely set at market open (4/24) and is roughly 1.5pp BELOW the then-current VoteHub level. The market is structured 'will approval go up this week?' but actually resolves on absolute crossing of 38.7, not on direction. So the real question: will VoteHub average drop to <=38.7 by 4/30? Current VoteHub estimate ~41.5 (consistent with 43% approve from cited net -11.8 and slight reweighting). To cross down to 38.7 within 3 days requires a ~2.8pp drop in a slow-moving time-weighted average — extremely unlikely without a single huge low-approval poll dominating the kernel. With sigma 0.6pp on 3-day VoteHub forward, P(X > 38.7) = 1 - Phi((38.7 - 41.2)/0.6) = 1 - Phi(-4.17) ~ 0.99997. Cap at 0.99. However, I am uncertain about (a) the exact threshold construction — 38.7 might be set differently than I assume, (b) whether VoteHub really is at 41+ today vs. closer to 39-40, in which case the math tightens. Apply moderate haircut for anchor uncertainty: P(YES) = 0.85.
notes: Market last 0.70, yes_bid 0.70, yes_ask 0.71. The market is implying ~30% chance VoteHub drops below 38.7 by 4/30. That is pricing in a much steeper crash than the polling-aggregator dynamics suggest. HOWEVER significant caveats: (1) Could not directly fetch VoteHub site (403). My anchor of ~41.5 comes from indirect news citations of net approval -11.8 with ~43% approve in mid-late April. If VoteHub is actually at ~39.5 today (more in line with FPO 37 and SB 39), then sigma 0.6 makes P(X>38.7) ~91% — still bullish but tighter. (2) The Reuters/Ipsos 33% and AP-NORC 33% polls landing this week (4/27-28) WILL pull VoteHub down. If three sub-35 polls hit the kernel, a 1.5pp drop is plausible. (3) The 38.7 threshold being so far below the current value is the key signal — Kalshi typically sets these thresholds near current, so 38.7 may reflect VoteHub at ~39 not 41 right now. (4) Market price 0.70 and the strike-set inferred current value imply VoteHub IS already near 38.7-39.5 territory, not 41. ADJUSTING DOWN: anchor revised to current ~40.0 with sigma 0.7 → P(X>38.7) = 1-Phi(-1.86) = 96.9%. Average my two estimates and apply uncertainty haircut: 0.85 fair. Net: BUY YES at 0.71 has positive EV. Conf medium-low because of anchor uncertainty.
